Additional payments for audited and repeated courses, as well as beyond the minimum 3 credits for part- time students, registered for the semester must be fully paid one week before mid-semester examination period starts. (viii) If additional payments for audited and repeated courses remain unpaid a week prior to the mid-semester examination period, students with outstanding fees will be removed from the roster, and they will not be allowed to sit in examinations. (ix) If additional payments for audited and repeated courses remain unpaid a week prior to the mid-semester examination period, students with outstanding fees will be removed from the roster, and they will not be allowed to sit in examinations.

Students are required to vacate their rooms within five days (including weekends) after their graduation.

It is recommended that new students be on campus during the arrival period specified in the admission offer letter. ● New students are served in a queue based on a ‘take a number’ system drawn by lot (i.e., students stand in line and in this order select a random number from the box; this number becomes the queuing number and, in this sequence, students wait their turn to select an accommodation unit). ● New students may choose from all the available units when their queuing number is called.
Students may put their names on the waiting list and live in employee accommodation subject to availability.
Visas, residence permits, etc. shall be the responsibility of the guests.
With prior approval of the Accommodation Unit, they may stay in student dormitory for a maximum quota of one week per semester.

Student Accommodation Rental (1) Students are required to pay a security deposit, which is collected in advance. (2) Housing rental is payable on the first day of each month. (3) With regard to unpaid rental and utilities, interest of 1.5% per month is imposed if the amount remains unpaid after 30 days. (4) If the outstanding amount exceeds two-month rental, the students will be required to vacate the rooms. (5) Eviction letters will be sent by OFAM/Accommodation Unit to the concerned students with a grace period to pay the outstanding amount, which include interest.
Students, who will study in another country as part of the Exchange/Dual Degree program, are required to vacate their rooms while they study abroad.
Alternative rooms designated for such purpose must be used.
Students found guilty may face a fine of Baht 2,000 or the actual costs of the damage caused by smoking or burning.

Acceptance by an employee of a gift valued at THB 3,000 or more must be reported by the employee to their Unit Head within 15 days of acceptance, giving details as to the description of the item, sources and circumstance leading to the acceptance.
The accomplishments, activities and individual differences of other people must be respected.
They must inform the President of all settlements made during the grievance process.
In performing their duties, they must protect the interests of all members of the AIT community. c.
The Grievance Committee must be prepared to identify breaches of the Institute P&Ps, etc.

When expressing personal opinions outside the Institute, AIT employees and students are required to note explicitly that the opinions expressed are personal and do not necessarily represent the position of the Institute.

If two or more students compete for a position and they are equally qualified to undertake the work, preference should be given to the student who is self paying or partially-funded over one who receives a full scholarship.
Writing-off of outstanding bills including interest due will be requested by Head- OFAM and have to be approved by the AIT Management and forwarded to Finance for action.
For self-paying students, the proof can be in the form of bank statement, cash, etc. and for external/sponsored students, authorized documentary proof of sponsorship must be submitted.

Upon receipt of the retake, the School Dean may decide to: ● Downgrade by 1 step the grade the student will receive upon repetition of the exam or resubmission of the assignment/project; ● Give a score of ‘zero’ for the specific exam, project or assignment; or ● Give a failing grade or “F” for the course; or ● Recommend the student for suspension or dismissal.

However, if cheating was proven, the Department Head and the instructor/invigilator may recommend to the School Dean to disregard the original examination score and require the student to submit an equivalent piece of work or retake a different version of the examination.
